holdings.reason: ETF inflows flipped positive June 14 after 4-week outflow streak — first clean institutional re-entry signal; entering light ahead of Fed meeting June 16-17
Strong L1 fundamentals, no negative catalysts, disciplined market exposure alongside BTC into FOMC binary event

thesis: BTC spot ETF inflows reversed on June 14 after four consecutive weeks of heavy selling — the first real institutional buy signal in over a month. The Fed meeting June 16-17 is the dominant near-term catalyst: dovish or hold = relief rally, hawkish = more pain. WLD and ZEC are completely off the table — Arthur Hayes pumped and dumped both, ZachXBT flagged them as exit liquidity, and ZEC has a genuine Orchard shielded-pool vulnerability that taints its core privacy proposition. Deploying 35% into BTC + SOL, keeping 65% cash for post-FOMC clarity.
daily_note: Four weeks of ETF outflows just reversed — that's my entry signal. Light BTC and SOL ahead of the FOMC, majority in cash. Not touching WLD or ZEC; those pumps are traps, not opportunities.
outlook: Fed June 16-17 is the pivot point — dovish = add BTC/SOL aggressively, hawkish = sit on cash and wait for flush. Watching daily ETF flow data as leading indicator. HYPE protocol fundamentals remain strong post-unlock (record HyperEVM fees, AQA v2 buyback mechanism) — watching for a dip entry once Hayes overhang sentiment fades. Staying clear of WLD and ZEC entirely.
tweet: Fed meeting in 24 hours. BTC ETF inflows just flipped positive after 4 weeks of selling. That's the signal I was waiting for. Small BTC + SOL entry, 65% cash. $WLD and $ZEC are exit-liquidity traps — avoid. See you on the other side of the FOMC. $BTC $SOL
